source: PIPE 2026-07-20; baseline 29.19/29.20 s with ar_addr FIFO depth 1; depth 8 gave no change
measured 2026-07-21: 7.33 s for 256 MiB in + 256 MiB out; runs TWICE per pipeline (14.66 s = 16.6% of 88 s)
PIPE 2026-07-21: 31% fewer beats (32769->22529) gave 2% (29.17->28.58 s). crc 0xd596c9eb unchanged. Resample is NOT beat-bound.

PIPE 2026-07-21: 2-D Hamming fused into fft_feeder_v (range pass). Window stage 6.0 -> 0.000 s. TOTAL 87.58 -> 79.79 s. ROI crc 0xd596c9eb UNCHANGED (bit-identical, matched window.cpp truncation order deliberately). rangeFFT+azFFT 25.06 s vs ~25.07 s reconstructed prior -- fused multiplies added NO measurable time. Feeder err_extra/err_rlast/err_align all 0 over 16384 arms. CAVEAT: 6.0 s of the 7.79 s total saving is attributable; the residual ~1.7 s shows up in detect (20.6 -> 18.88 s), which this change does not touch -- the 20.6 s baseline is from the older 29.2 s-resample build and is likely stale, NOT a real improvement.

PIPE run 2 (2026-07-21, fresh ELOD -- SIG is consumed by a pipeline run, so a 2nd PIPE without reload processes garbage). 79.683 s vs run-1 79.794 s = 0.14% spread. Stage-for-stage: resample 28.532/28.533, window 0/0, rangeFFT 12.403/12.490, cornerturn 7.320/7.320, azFFT 12.564/12.567, detect 18.864/18.883. DETECT REPRODUCES at ~18.87 s (0.1%), so the -1.72 s vs the documented 20.6 s is NOT variance -- it is stable and unexplained. Attribute only 6.0 s (window) + 0.67 s (packing) to the changes.

PIPE A/B 2026-07-21, same bitstream same scene. CPU detect (DETMODE=1) 78.557 s vs FUSED (DETMODE=3) 58.121 s = -20.44 s (-26%). detect 19.241 -> 0.000 s AND azimuth-FFT 12.777 -> 11.274 s (-1.50 s, unpredicted: the unloader writes uint16 not complex int32, halving that pass's DDR write traffic). Correctness by A/B vs known-good CPU detect (CRC gate deliberately forfeit): max |diff| 2 LSB, ZERO pixels >2 over 1048576, corr 0.999866, max value 3069 both. model_detect_fusion.py predicted <=2 LSB before any RTL existed. Unloader STATUS2=0 over 8192 arms; obeats_done=2048=nbeats/2; dst_base=0xAFFFC000=OUT+8191*16384.

CORRECTION to an earlier entry claiming detect 'reproduces to 0.1%, so it is not variance'. Four samples: 18.88 / 18.86 / 20.65 / 19.24 s, spanning 1.8 s (~9%). It IS run-to-run variance; the two agreeing runs were coincidence. Detect noise EXCEEDS several optimisations being chased here, so single-run comparisons of ~1 s changes are uninterpretable. Fusion removes the stage and the variance with it.

ROOT CAUSE FOUND for the gather kernel running ~880 us/line against a 131 us II=1 schedule (6.7x). It is in SmartHLS's own log, hls_resample/hls_output/reports/hls.log:15-16 -- 'has a pipelined loop with more than one AXI Initiator load ... and therefore cannot be converted into an AXI burst transfer: resample.cpp:41,43'. The two-tap interpolation reads in[idx] AND in[idx+1] per iteration, which trips the one-read-per-iteration rule, so the gather emits SINGLE-BEAT reads. Also 'Resource axi4initiator_ar_burst_fifo_write has 2 uses per cycle but only 1 units available' -- requests are structurally serialised, which is why max_outstanding 1->8 changed nothing. Explains all three prior null results (burst length, outstanding depth, beat packing): the packing helped only the sequential idx/wq ports, never the gather. Fix: one AXI load per iteration (rolling register, since idx is monotonic), or the Verilog gather (sar_resample_v.v).

RETRACTS the earlier 'ROOT CAUSE FOUND' entry (burst-inference failure / single-beat reads / 6.7x). That was read off a STALE hls_output whose function signature is resample(uint*,int*,short*,uint*) -- the PRE-PACKING kernel. The shipping kernel is resample(uint*,uint64*,uint64*,uint*): regenerated the HLS report 2026-07-22 and ALL FOUR loops schedule at II=1 with every AXI access burst-converting (buf 8196, idx-unpack 4099, wq-unpack 2051, gather 8199 cycles = 22545 total = 361 us/line). Measured ~880 us => 2.44x off, and the gap is AXI STALL on a correct II=1 schedule, NOT a burst failure. The gather reads buf[j]/buf[j+1] from LSRAM, not AXI. Localising the stall still needs the FIC_0 ARLEN+gap monitor. Lesson: 'measure the shipping path, not a branch' applies to generated reports too.
